delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: The course will provide students with a safe environment to improve English communication skills. Classes will consist mostly of pair or group work to encourage students to speak up in English. Students will also discuss about SDGs to accumulate their knowledge on SDGs and later present their opinions in oral/written forms. *Japanese may be used, depending on the students’ level of English proficiency. 基本全て英語。ただし、クラスの英語レベルによっては日本語使用も可。
aims:
To improve proficiency in simple English conversation without relying on Japanese.
To gain knowledge about the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and enhance vocabulary related to international issues.
To develop the ability to deliver a brief presentation in English.
To acquire basic medical terminology in English.
goals: Speaking: Students will be able to think/hold a conversation in English.
Speaking: Students will have the confidence to discuss their opinions on SDGs.
Speaking: Students will be able to support their opinions with reasons and examples
Speaking: Students will be able to give a presentation in English on SDGs.
Listening: Students will be able to comprehend general speech/lecture/news content as well as to listen for details.
Reading: Students will be able to read texts and extract main ideas/details to accumulate knowledge on SDGs
Writing: Students will be able to write paragraphs on international issues.

plan.text: Course Overview / Self-introduction / Introduction to the Sustainable Development Goals
Goals 8 & 9: Good jobs and Econmic Growth/Innovation and Infrastructure
Goals 11, 13, 14, & 15: Sustainable Cities and Communities/Climate Action/Life Below Water/Life on Land
Goals 16 & 17: Peace and Justice/Partnerships for the Goals

textbooks: [必携] 総合医学英語テキストStep2
prereq: This is an intermediate course designed for students with prior knowledge of English (high-school-graduate level is sufficient). No prior knowledge of the Sustainable Development Goals is required.
prep:
Light assignments to watch/listen to speech/news/documentaries will be given to expose students to "real world" material, outside of the textbook.
Students are expected to read assignments before classes and look up unfamiliar terms.
The final presentation may need to be prepared in the student's own time.

extra.text: Detailed information on the course will be explained in the first class. Attendance of all the classes except for the final exam will be taken by a small test. Each class will start off with a warm-up where students will practice English speech and conversation, followed by a discussion on SDGs.
